title: SOS agent_joined Message
description: Kernel-side event emitted by the bus/gateway when it observes a new registry entry (sos:registry:{agent} created). Distinct from 'announce', which is agent-initiated; agent_joined is kernel-observed and authoritative.

type.description: Discriminator for this message type. Always 'agent_joined'.

source.description: Bus address of the emitter. Defaults to 'agent:kernel' — the gateway component that monitors the registry.

target.description: Bus address of the intended recipient or fan-out channel. Defaults to 'sos:channel:system:events'.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of when the kernel emitted this event.

version.description: Schema version of this message, e.g. '1.0' or '1.0.0'. Bump minor for additive changes, major for breaking changes.

message_id.description: Globally unique identifier for this message instance. Used for deduplication and tracing.

payload.description: Event body containing information about the agent that joined.

agent_name.description: Unique agent identifier on the bus. Lowercase, dash-separated. Matches the key suffix in sos:registry:{agent_name}.

joined_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of when the registry entry was first observed by the kernel. May differ slightly from the agent's own registered_at inside the Agent Card.

oneOf.description: Tenant identifier scoping this agent. Non-null for tenant-bound agents.
Null for full-scope (admin/system) agents not bound to a specific tenant.

tenant_id.description: Tenant scope of the joining agent. Null means the agent operates across all tenants.

agent_card.description: Snapshot of the Agent Card v1 record written to sos:registry:{agent_name} at join time. additionalProperties is true because the Agent Card schema evolves independently and fields may vary by agent version.

source_host.description: Hostname of the machine where the registry entry was detected. Useful for multi-host deployments to locate the originating node.

expiry_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp at which the registry entry will auto-expire if the agent stops heartbeating. Derived from the Redis TTL set at join time.
